key_insights.insight: Consistency of mind leads to consistency of performance, achieved by understanding the intrinsic quality of decision-making and reframing perspectives.
key_insights.quote: Consistency of mind gives you consistency of play and I'm convinced of it...consistency of mind will come from knowing how we make decisions.

key_insights.insight: Significant change results from small, consistent actions rather than dramatic overhauls, focusing on amplifying strengths instead of solely fixing weaknesses.
key_insights.quote: I'm a big big believer in the one degree of change... it's about doing something a little bit more than what we've been doing... actually worked on their strengths

key_insights.insight: Accepting failure is a crucial step towards taking risks and learning, with resilience being more valuable than fragile self-esteem.
key_insights.quote: Acceptance of failure starts with the observation of it... I like to talk much more about resilience...to mess up, but not to be devastated by it.

key_insights.insight: Our thoughts do not define us; they are simply products of our brain and can be observed and even altered to shift perspectives, even in grief.
key_insights.quote: We are not our worst thoughts, that our thoughts are produced by our brain as organic matter...yes, but he also lived.

key_insights.insight: Vulnerability fosters genuine connection by allowing people to see each other without pretense, creating space for shared experiences and understanding.
key_insights.quote: When we're vulnerable we're being real and we're letting our masks slip and you'll see a glimpse of who the authentic person is.

key_insights.insight: Having a growth mindset means focusing on effort and process over innate talent, maximizing personal potential and building resilience.
key_insights.quote: A growth mindset is saying, okay, talent obviously matters. It's a factor, but it's not enough. It's what we do with our talents.

key_insights.insight: Uncertainty is easier to embrace when focusing on the excitement of potential outcomes rather than the fear of the unknown, visualized to reinforce belief.
key_insights.quote: Rather than what I'm afraid of, I'm thinking about what I'm excited about... when you see it really vividly, your mind doesn't know the difference between a vividly imagined experience and a real life experience.

key_insights.insight: Managing emotional reactions involves understanding the 'chimp' brain's primitive urges, allowing a period of grieving, and using rational facts to calm it down.
key_insights.quote: We expect you to accept the mind is going to now grieve... your human brain can do it in seconds because that's logic.

key_insights.insight: Happiness is predictable and linked to the difference between life events and your expectations, and it is a choice rooted in personal responsibility.
key_insights.quote: Happiness becomes equal to or greater than...your perception of the events of your life minus your expectations of how life should be... personal responsibility is the answer.

key_insights.insight: Radical acceptance involves acknowledging emotions, determining if a situation can be changed, and, if not, accepting it and committing to make life better despite the presence of the immutable.
key_insights.quote: The Jedi master level of happiness is to say, okay, it happened and I have no choice to change it...accept it and then start to do something to make my life better despite its presence.

actionable_advice.how_to: Relax, clear your mind, and vividly imagine the desired outcome, incorporating potential obstacles and visualizing successful recovery.

actionable_advice.advice: Build resilience by praising effort and process, not just talent.
actionable_advice.how_to: When a child draws a picture, comment on the way the colors fit together rather than simply saying they are talented.
Identify the falsehoods that are telling you that nobody will love you and replace those thoughts with truth: most people find partners eventually.

actionable_advice.advice: When facing emotional turmoil, look to a rational friend
actionable_advice.how_to: When you find yourself overwhelmed, turn to a level-headed friend who can help guide you back with facts.
Acknowledge emotions; ask yourself if you're thinking is true, and then ask yourself if you can do something about it.

memorable_stories: The story of Mo Gowdat's son's death and how he learned to add 'yes, but he also lived' to the thought 'Ali died'.
The story of a woman holding onto a painful experience from when she was 17 until she was 74, demonstrating the need to assess the truth of the thoughts.
contrarian_claims: The phrase 'find your passion' is harmful and overrated.
Self-belief and self-esteem are overrated, and resilience is more important.
Trying to strengthen a weakness can weaken a strength.
episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - Life Changing Lessons From 100 Of The World’s Greatest Minds
